2605.04330.title: The Scaling Properties of Implicit Deductive Reasoning in Transformers
2605.04330.paper_summary: We investigate the scaling properties of implicit deductive reasoning over Horn clauses in depth-bounded Transformers. By systematically decorrelating provability from spurious features and enforcing algorithmic alignment, we find that in sufficiently deep models with a bidirectional prefix mask, implicit reasoning approaches explicit CoT performance across graph topologies and problem widths, though CoT remains necessary for depth extrapolation.
2605.04330.paper_summary_zh: 我們深入研究了在深度受限的Transformer中，隱式推理對於霍恩子句的縮放特性。通過系統性地將可證性與虛假特徵去相關並強化算法對齊，我們發現，在具有雙向前綴掩碼的足夠深的模型中，隱式推理在各種圖拓撲和問題寬度上接近顯式的鏈接推理性能，儘管鏈接推理在深度外推中仍然是必要的。
2605.04330.author: Enrico Vompa et.al.
